text.title: 7 Idea Generation Frameworks
text.summary: Seven systematic frameworks for generating business ideas, with templates and worked examples for each.

rule.statement: Start by mapping technical, domain, and soft skills; then ask what problems each skill cluster can solve — not what products you could build.
rule.rationale: Problem-first thinking from existing skills avoids the "solution looking for a problem" failure mode. You already have the domain credibility to execute.

example.code: Skill: Python + Marketing analytics Problem: Small businesses have no affordable analytics automation Idea: Automated social media analytics tool for small businesses

section.title: Framework 2: Pain Point Mining
section.p: Search Reddit (sort by "top" in niche subreddits), Twitter/X rants, App Store 1-2 star reviews, Quora questions, and forum complaints. For each pain point, document: Pain / Frequency / Current Solutions / Gap / Idea.

rule.statement: List tedious tasks people pay others to do (Upwork gigs, Fiverr, outsourced small-business work) and apply the formula: "Automate [repetitive task] for [specific audience]".
rule.rationale: People who hire for repetitive work have already proven willingness to pay. Automating what they outsource produces a validated demand signal.

section.title: Framework 4: Productized Service
section.p: Turn services into products: identify the repeatable part of a service you or others provide, then package it. Consulting → Course. Custom dev → SaaS template. Design work → Design system.

rule.statement: Identify one specific use case of a large platform (Excel, Notion, Salesforce) that deserves a focused tool; build only that.
rule.rationale: Large platforms are designed for breadth, not depth. Niche users are underserved and willing to pay for a tool that actually fits their workflow.

section.title: Framework 6: Market Stacking
section.p: Combine two niches: Niche A x Niche B = New opportunity. Examples: AI x Legal = Contract analysis. Fitness x Remote work = Home workout for developers. Crypto x Freelancing = Payment platform.

antipattern.description: Defaulting to "AI x [anything]" without genuine domain fit or a specific underserved user problem.
antipattern.reason: LLMs over-produce this pattern. "AI-powered X" is not a business idea; it is a feature. The underlying job must be validated independently.

rule.statement: Document problems you face daily; if you have the problem and can't find a solution, assume others do too.
rule.rationale: Your own problems give you authentic domain knowledge, built-in motivation, and access to a test audience (your network).

text.reference: Session template with per-idea fields and top-3 to validate
Weekly log for ongoing problem and complaint capture
